:::{#def-collapsibility}

#### Collapsibility

An estimand $\theta$ is **collapsible** with respect to a covariate $Z$
if the population-level estimand equals the marginal-distribution-weighted average
of stratum-specific estimands:

$$
\ba
\theta
&= \E{\theta(Z)}
\\
&= \int \theta(z)\, f_Z(z)\, dz.
\ea
$$

**Collapsibility bias** is the discrepancy that arises when $\theta \neq \E{\theta(Z)}$.

:::

@exm-collapsibility below works through a numerical case.

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_thm-collapsibility.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_exm-collapsibility.qmd >}}

{{< slidebreak >}}

{{< include _subfiles/logistic-regression/_sec-logistic-marginal-considerations.qmd >}}
